answer.startPath: create a downstream business package or service
answer.reason: business capabilities are registered through public capability manifests and projected into platform contracts

businessCapability.registration: append CatalogManifest() in the downstream composition root before resolving enabled capabilities

adminSurface.projection: capability.Manifest.Admin.Resources -> admin resource contract -> menu, permissions, schema and settings aggregation

deployment.package: build a downstream API binary or service image that includes the business capability manifest
deployment.enable: select platform capabilities plus example-catalog in the downstream desired capability list
deployment.restart: regenerate contracts and restart the API process; v1 does not hot-load external packages

upgradeStrategy.platform: upgrade the platform module version, run contract validators, then rebuild the downstream composition root
upgradeStrategy.business: bump the capability semver, keep resource keys and permission prefixes stable, add idempotent migrations for data changes
upgradeStrategy.rollback: roll back the downstream binary and desired capability set; persisted business data remains owned by the business package
